// all_features.iff.txt — comprehensive iffcomp torture test.
//
// Exercises every production in lang.y that doesn't require environment
// setup. Specifically excluded:
//   - include <...>      — requires $WF_DIR to be set
//   - include "..."      — tested separately by include_basic.iff.txt
//   - negative literals  — the grammar accepts `-42` but the writer's
//                          range check rejects them on 64-bit (%ld → u64
//                          produces huge values that exceed every width
//                          bound). Use hex literals for "negative-looking"
//                          bytes. Same limitation in C++, Go, and Rust.
//
// Alignment invariant: **nested chunks must start at 4-aligned positions**.
// The C++ library computes `cs.pos = tellp() + 4` at enterChunk time (before
// out_id's align(4) runs), so if the parent's current position isn't
// already 4-aligned, the child's cs.pos is stale and the exit_chunk
// assertion `pos == cs.pos + cs.size + 4` fires. The fix is authorial:
// put all nested chunks at the top of their parent's payload, before any
// variable-length items (strings, file-includes) that could leave a
// non-aligned position. Variable-length items come after nested chunks.
//
// The .timestamp section inside 'TIME' is masked by the test harness
// because the value is time-varying.

{ 'ROOT'

  // ==================================================================
  // Part 1: Everything that must be written at 4-aligned positions.
  //         All nested chunks go here, before any variable-length data.
  // ==================================================================

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 1. Nested chunks at depth > 1, with FOURCC-as-item at aligned
  //    positions (right after int32 writes).
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  { 'NEST'
    { 'DEEP'
      42l
      'INNR'                                // aligned: follows 4-byte int32
    }
    66l
  }

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 2. Backward references: NEST is closed, so the writer resolves
  //    these immediately. Each emits 4 bytes, position stays aligned.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  .sizeof(::'ROOT'::'NEST')                  // 4 bytes
  .offsetof(::'ROOT'::'NEST')                // 4 bytes
  .offsetof(::'ROOT'::'NEST', 8)             // 4 bytes, with integer addend

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 3. Forward references: LATE isn't closed yet, so the writer queues
  //    back-patches and fills them in at end-of-parse.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  .sizeof(::'ROOT'::'LATE')                  // 4 bytes
  .offsetof(::'ROOT'::'LATE')                // 4 bytes

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 4. LATE — target of the forward references above. Contents: a
  //    FOURCC-as-item at chunk start (aligned) plus a hex int32.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  { 'LATE'
    'ENDD'                                   // aligned: start of payload
    $12345678l
  }

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 5. .timestamp — non-deterministic, masked by the test harness.
  //    Isolated in its own chunk so the masker can find it by FOURCC.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  { 'TIME' .timestamp }

  // ==================================================================
  // Part 2: Everything that leaves the position non-aligned. No nested
  //         chunks allowed below this line.
  // ==================================================================

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 6. Integer widths: default (1 byte), explicit [ywl] suffix, and
  //    { Y | W | L } state-push blocks (which change the default only;
  //    they do not emit chunk headers, so alignment is irrelevant).
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  0 1 2 255                                  // default 1-byte width
  0y 1w 2l                                   // explicit suffix
  { Y 10 20 30 }                             // push default = 1
  { W 1000 2000 3000 }                       // push default = 2
  { L 100000 200000 }                        // push default = 4

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 7. Hex integer literals with width suffix
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  $DEy $BEEFw $7ACEBABEl

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 8. Numeric expressions. `+` and `-` evaluate their operands and
  //    discard the result (the grammar's chunk_statement → expr action
  //    is empty). The items on each side still emit their bytes in
  //    order: `5l + 3l - 2l` writes 5, 3, 2 as LE int32.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  5l + 3l - 2l

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 9. Reals: default precision, explicit override, integer-as-real
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  3.14(1.15.16)                              // explicit (S.W.F) override
  .5(1.15.16)                                // leading-dot form
  42(1.15.16)                                // integer mantissa → real

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 10. Precision state push
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  { .precision(1.7.8)
    1.5 0.25
  }

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 11. Strings: plain, padded, concatenated, escaped
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  "plain string"
  "padded"(16)                               // NUL-term + zero-pad to 16
  "con" "cat" "enation"                      // string_list concatenation
  "esc\n\t\\\"\065\066"                      // \n \t \\ \" \DDD escapes

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 12. Alignment with custom fill character. The 5y above leaves us
  //     non-aligned on purpose so the align has something to pad.
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  5y
  .fillchar(170)                             // 0xAA
  .align(16)

  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  // 13. File inclusion: full file and sliced range
  // ------------------------------------------------------------------
  [ "TODO" ]
  [ "TODO" .start(10) .length(20) ]
}
